txt: *Especially* that guy. That guy's the most compromised one in here.
*Now* you guys suddenly have theories pouring out? When they're obviously just stabs in the dark...
*Or* you could *not* make this about corruption and go with something even wilder.
*Pussy-boy?* What the hell is going on here? That does not sound trustworthy at all.
*Very* off. Just let her go. Listen to me for once...
*You* told him to do that stupid thing...
...*all* of them.
...is what you want to do. But should you? He expressly stated you shouldn't hear about it. What if it renders you mad? Or catatonic? *Or* makes you lose your memory again?
...no? He's not going to show up? I'm sorry, your lie detection isn't working. It's not her doing, he's just totally inept. It looks like you're also an idiot. But that's not her fault.
...or maybe you should take her to the station? For safekeeping. She *lied* to you. And she's a flight risk.
...or should we arrest her? She's a flight risk and she lied to you -- she should be taken into custody.
...with the sort of glee that's meant to imply you've already taken on the case.
A cool, damp feeling ripples through you. You realise you needn't have rolled over quite so easily.
A dull despair is creeping into her voice.
A lifetime of smoking and drinking will do that to you.
A little. They're all still of limited use, interpreting things to the best of their ability. Maybe they add flair or something? I wouldn't know. I don't add flair.
A step forward for technology, yes -- but only for those who can afford it.
A typical power play. Wait for him to speak first -- show him you've got a backbone.
A wrong has been made right. You may rest easy now.
Absolutely not.
Absolutely. It could have come from *anywhere*. But you're suddenly so certain it came from the roof behind the window.
Accept it. The body is an object and objects break down. Do what good you can with yours, before the rest goes too.
Alright, come on now. If *he* hasn't said anything about your lack of pants, no one will. You're only hurting yourself by not wearing them...
Alright, maybe not *healthier*, but it's a *bonus* that you've drunk so hard you can't remember any of your past relationships. Oblivion's the ace in your corner.
An attempt to pander to your perceived conservative sensibilities. No person his age would ever use a word like *narcomania* with a straight face. Don't fall for it!
An enormous expenditure of willpower to build up strategic semen reserves? You had me at *willpower*. Let's do it!
And then he lost it all by going for a jog... not exactly *inspiring*.
And then it hits you.
And you've already lost for so long.
And your mind healing too. Weaving itself back into shape.
Any plan to call him Pinball or Kimball is immediately wiped from your neocortex, as if with some sort of mind altering device. It is simply not going to happen.
Anything strike you a bit off about this mish-mash?
Are you *stupid*?
Are you *sure* lieutenant? For some reason, you have a little doubt about that...
Are you positive that what you're doing here is proper police conduct? You can still leave and do *nothing*.
Are you serious? You sure you don't want to hazard a guess here, detective?
Are you? Because it sort of feels like you *are*. Please don't cry.
As she has to -- every day.
As usual, he does not respond to the political agitation.
As you wait, your breathing slows down. Some mosquito-like organism buzzes in your peripheral:
Ask about that 'cock on parade' too. Make sure you get everything here.
Asking too many questions will make you look weak. You should maybe focus on the folder.
Be *professional*. He probably doesn't even know what happened to you, why the strike is over...
Be very very precise now... don't make a splash, you can still mess it up.
Because it's an anti-radiation drug and you're a cop, not a post-apocalyptic scavenger?
Because it's just not you. I'm sorry.
Being Cuno's pig has a steadying effect on your hand. Go with the flow, man...
Believe it.
Besides, you've got a case to solve right now, and you won't get to solve it if you turn yourself into a lab rat. Whatever's wrong with you can wait.
Blissful idiot -- say something. You've stood there for too long.
But he can now.
But it isn't. And you shouldn't come back to this anymore. This should be the last time. Stop talking about that damn window, please.
But not too long. This is important to her. She has the courage to ask.
But that would mean he *doesn't* say every pathetic thing that pops in his head.
But when it comes to assessments of character and factual accuracy they are not to be trusted. Not with her.
But you don't even *want* to talk to her! She would only be cold and mean. Let her go.
C'mon. It's so juvenile. Besides, there's no way this man is going to get it.
C'mon. She lied through her teeth. And you know it.
Calm down. Anyone watching can see you're doing nothing. Just keep it professional.
Chin up. Keep focusing on the positives here.
Come now. Humans have earned your trust. And you theirs...
Concentrate on the belt, not on who is  whose pig. There is no pig. There are only the chaincutters -- and the belt.
DON'T GO INTO SHOCK. HOLD ON.
Damn... that's a nasty play. He's got you -- a titan of Volition -- in a fork. Guess you just have to sit or leave.
Despair creeps into you, getting fat on your weakness. Whatever noble intentions you once had as a police officer -- it's eating them all up now.
Despite the stonewalling you can slip one more question in.
Ding, ding, ding! We have a winner!
Do as she says. There's no other option.
Does this thing *unmake* you, or does it heal you?
Doesn't look like he has a nervous breakdown every two seconds.
Doing some *good* will alleviate the hangover. At least you're of use to *someone*.
Don't ask, don't look, don't do *anything* here. Just go away. Get back to work.
Don't be an idiot and say it. In this day and age, of all times. It won't end well.
Don't be distracted by the flattery and funny-man act. *Questions*.
Don't be melodramatic. You can trust them. Just not with *her*.
Don't be paranoid, she's obviously talking about someone else, not you.
Don't be ridiculous. It's just a regular person, probably a worker from the harbour.
Don't do that. Stay strong. The hangover will wear off. You don't need to keep doing this to yourself.
Don't fall for it, it's a ploy to get you talking. The honey-mouthed syllables, the smiling, the conspiratorial jokes, all of it!
Don't focus on the pain -- focus on doing your job. Tell her she's under arrest.
Don't get emotional.
Don't give up -- but don't leave either. You're going to have this conversation on your terms. You just need to remain headstrong.
Don't let her scare you. She probably just made that name up on the spot.
Don't listen. Just go.
Don't overdo it. It's okay to be unsure.
Don't panic. I'm sure he's just doing his shoelaces up. Something like that.
Don't react. Whatever you do, don't react to that last thing.
Don't worry, it's only been four or five seconds. You've got this.
Don't. Everyone is a whore? What does that even mean? Breathe in and walk away.
Don't.
Ease off it. This was not an unmitigated disaster. The lieutenant mitigated it. Just proceed, calmly...
Easy. Easy now.
Excuse me, I'm not sure I heard that properly...
Executive function is completely shot. I keep pushing buttons and nothing is happening.
Extremely rigid principles this old soldier has, don't leave room for a change of heart or backing out. The medal is yours.
First the investigation, now this... how many more things do you have to fuck up?
For a second her face disappears from your cornea. Only a silvery negative remains.
For all his passion, this man is diligent and patient. You could learn things from him.
Fortunately, saying sorry is practically second-nature for you.
Four simple words -- thank god. If he can see, then you're not insane.
Free from self-awareness. No deliberation, only -- and I mean *only* -- execution.
Fun? You need to stop. Harry. You're killing yourself.
Get a hold of yourself just once, will you -- try and look past the *sexually* lax morals and do some police work.
Get a hold of yourself, this is just one little thing. You still have everything else! Go on, finish the interrogation!
Get a hold of yourself. He needs your support now too.
Get over it, proceed!
Get yourself together. It's just police work.
Go.
Good idea. Just ask him. He won't make a scene.
Good idea.
Good on you. I know you were tempted to shout *fuck*, but you controlled your impulse. Well done.
Good point. It is rather rare these days... But do continue.
Good. The less paranoid option. You can trust them. Just not with *her*.
Great -- after you've done this, you can finally get to the island. Let's wrap this up!
Great -- you *do know* that we can just abandon this mission and go to the island? Let's close this case!
Guess you held your ground. It can be good, being honest with yourself.
Hang on to the story -- veterans get sentimental after such *retellings* -- this might yield something useful.
Harry... I know there's not much to say -- but if nothing else, just remember that you've made it this far. And it's just a bit farther now. Let's finish this.
Harry... we're just going to store this one as a sellable item, okay? We're not going to let you kill yourself by drinking it. That's just not an option. Sell it at the pawnshop for profit.
He can't go. Not before the case is solved.
He cleans his hands of it. It's too much.
He did not win. There is a crack in there now -- and it's spreading across the face of his certainty.
He doesn't *want* to see her. It's simple as that.
He doesn't exactly come across as responsible. Are you sure you can trust him with your *entire fortune*?
He has lost the will to live.
He hasn't been in your room, relax.
He is a brave man -- why is he unravelling?
He isn't 'working on it' -- he'll never stop drinking.
He just *couldn't*...
He means it. This is the end of him talking to you.
He subdues the feeling. Dusts himself off and moves on. So should you. There will be other chances.
He wants to argue, disagree -- but feels he's got no right, being at the mercy of Mr. Claire.
He was clearly uncomfortable discussing it before. He still is. You have nothing to gain from pushing his buttons.
He wasn't even using soap... *incredible*.
He would sooner die than let it surface.
He's *really* not. Just take it off him.
He's calm now. Threw all that turmoil away and became himself again.
He's dead set on this.
He's hiding. These things are tough on him. Matters of love, not violence or deceit.
He's not backing up from this. Standing up here is merely a practice run without paying him first.
He's not okay. This is just another black day, in a row of black days -- something *strange* is keeping him together, making him endure...

He's right. All those sweet questions waiting to be answered... you can choose to let them go, though. There will be plenty of questions to ask at another time. Or even from other people -- from people on the *island*.
He's right. All those sweet questions waiting to be answered... you can choose to let them go, though. There will be plenty of questions to ask at another time. Or even from other people.
He's right. Dust yourself off and move on. There will be other chances.
He's right. There are no airtight theories, just paranoia. You've given it some thought, now let it go.
He's used to this sort of thing.
Hell yeah!
Her! Stop letting her distract you.
Here come the bad vibes again. Relax. Distance yourself from it.
Here we go. For once you seem to have her off-guard.
Here's an idea... *Don't* think about shutting this twelve year old down. Stop asking him questions. Just walk away and cool off.
His pleaful smile is disarming, but you can withstand it's glorious assault, if you just put your heart into it.
His trust is well placed. You aren't. You can *feel* it.
His way to deal with this is -- at this point -- not to care. Do what you want.
Hmm... wow. She really isn't his taste. Her spell hasn't spread here.
Hmmm... *perhaps*?
How is this brave man unravelling?
How it always does -- through subtlety.
Huh... no, Mr. Conclusion. You're always kind of limited in your analytical abilities. That's not *her* fault. But still...
I am sorry. It just doesn't come out of your mouth. What does is:
I can't help you. I am totally useless. Everything I've said is lies. I want the exact same bad things you want. To stand here, like a pillar of salt, saying...
I can't restrain this one. The need to *cop* is too strong. You just need to ask it.
I don't know about this...
I have bad news for you.
I have no idea what's going on here, but you should not do anything that the horrific necktie tells you to do.
I take it back. He's got it pretty bad, but this *next guy's* on another level entirely...
I think it's safe to assume: yes. Mr. Thespian here hasn't been speaking up. If he were, I suspect there would be paeans to her truthfulness. Like this...
I told you it's not that bad.
I told you she kept lying to you. She's probably lying to someone else right now. In another city. She moves fast.
I told you she kept lying to you. She's probably lying to someone else right now. In another city.
I told you she kept on lying to you. She's probably lying to someone else right now -- another detective.
I wouldn't be so sure, detective.
I'm afraid it's not what *you* think it is either.
I'm sorry I didn't catch it sooner. It takes conscious effort on your part.
If it were true those organisms would *not* be working *in unison*.
If it's possible, then by pure willpower alone. You are going to have to become... a psycholocomotor.
If that good guy never comes out, what difference does it make?
If the lieutenant agrees, then maybe, just maybe -- you should pay attention?
Ignore her. She's not the main threat yet.
Ignore. Say no more. This is about the corpse and inspecting it.
In his defence -- to reduce him to such inadequacy, she probably had to employ half-truths more often than outright lies.
In honour of your shit, lieutenant-yefreitor. Which you kept *together* in the face of total, unrelenting terror. Day after day. Second by second.
In honour of your will, lieutenant-yefreitor. That you kept from falling apart, in the face of sheer terror. Day after day. Second by second.
Inspiration can go a long way in physical endeavours. You should try it again now.
Is he reading a book about his life so far to figure out which reality he's in?
Is it? Something is off here...
Isn't it strange to have your assumptions confirmed like this? This is what someone whispering *suggestions* in your ear would like you to feel.
Isn't it strange when all your assumptions are confirmed like this? Stood in a semicircle, hung him, carried him over... like you're supposed to be feeling clever. And content.
It also has startlingly little to do with arresting her, you suddenly realize...
It appears this one has *not* been entirely corrupted... this may help. This may help the others see.

It feels good to be the smart cop, doesn't it? That's a good cop to be. Has a nice ring to it. "Smart Cop." You wouldn't want to be "Stupid Cop," now would you? But still -- maybe he's just sucking up to you?
It is about a time to stop embarrassing yourself. Questions will help you regain some of your lost dignity.
It is ominous -- you already coming up with theories that put the blame on someone other than Klaasje.
It looks like this one's not waking up. You'll have to do it without pushing her further. At least for now.
It only lets you concentrate because you're in *nicotine withdrawal*. It only mitigates it's own...
It still feels as if this didn't *quite* go as it should have, but...
It takes a lot of willpower not to ask. Obviously he's been wanting to know what's behind the door...
It was me. I made you sound weak and I shouldn't have. But *this* guy, Goldmouth, he's way in over his head. Be careful with his so-called advice.
It will pass -- in time.
It's as if something *put* the thought there. To leave the flowers.
It's better to know you're being played than to be played without knowing it, is it not?
It's in there. She's just stonewalling.
It's like he re-conditioned your brain.
It's not *just* rudeness. It really is hard to concentrate on whatever she needs to do. And you're not helping.
It's not like you're going to gather any forces soon with all that drinking -- and he knows it.
It's not something he's proud of, but that's the way it is.
It's ominous -- you buying into her theory and running with it. Be careful with this Logic guy.
It's the *fault* of those who *killed* him. Not you.
It's time to leave it be. You're about to make a child cry. Are you proud of yourself?
It's, in a way, admirable how quickly he composes himself after such a blow. This man digs authority. Even when it's bullying him.
Just because you *can* doesn't mean you *have to* say everything that comes to mind. Back out before the situation escalates.
Just finish the case and you'll probably have somewhere to go. Hopefully.
Just have her answer the question. Don't give explanations.
Just leave it alone. He clearly doesn't want to share this with you.
Just let it all out. This might be your last chance.
Just like the last time, light brown eyes look back at you. All is as it was, you feel cigarette fumes rise from her lips. She's just as beautiful...
Just tell him the truth. Why does no one ever want to tell the truth?
Just wait. Relax.
Keep calm. Go over the whole situation in detail.
Keep it together, no need to show your wonder.
Keeping the habit within the parameters he's given himself takes a lot of focus. It would be easier to simply quit.
Kind of hard to sing a duet when you're all alone on-stage, isn't it, superstar?
Klaasje knew exactly what she was doing. You can't remain calm under pressure otherwise.
Let's cool Mr. Fight-Or-Flight down, before he kills someone. He gets overstimulated. But seriously, enough with the pétanque already.
Let's hope no one saw you wearing those outrageously regular -- almost moralist! -- pants.
Let's keep focusing on the drug trade. He was almost admitting to it.
Lieutenant Kitsuragi is a brave man for saying Madre's name without the winces and whispers that usually accompany it.
Like a marionette, on some invisible string...
Like hell you would have -- remember?
Listen to him. For once.
Listen... It's okay to take a few minutes to yourself. Sit down and have a breather.
Look at it, detective. And be ashamed. Until you make it right by *legally purchasing* that raincoat, I'll make sure you feel guilty every time you see it.
Look how blurry all the lines on these papers are -- how unwieldy your own willpower is to yourself. You're like an absurdist Samaran monk -- focusing through not focusing.
Look who's finally awake!
Look who's waking up from a thousand years of sleep!
Man, really? You're, like... just the most compromised of them all. Man, maybe you should just fire this one, it's completely useless.
Maybe it will make you temptation-proof, so something good will come out of this at least?
Maybe you *are* a moderately competent detective?
Maybe. Maybe not. Mercy is rarely a *complete* mistake.
Mhm, I know you do. These interisolary pants are like wearing a perfect *compromise* in your nether regions. No one will call the Moralintern on you like this, that's for sure.
Mm, these are some wonderfully regular pants. Not too tight, not too loose -- moderate in every sense. You'll blend right in at some pleasant dinner party.
More questions. Ask more.
Move on. There's no catching her this time around.
Multi-Face! I'm beginning to doubt your judgement -- are you sure you're not sleeping on the post? You have to wake this one up -- with force -- if you want to continue pushing her.
My god... maybe you *are*.
NO.
NOW!
Needless to say, this is another *move*. Don't take it.
No -- her first.
No one and nothing can change this man's mind -- he is as rigid as they come, still in that antique uniform -- it's a symbol for him.
No one can STOP you from finding the phasmid.
No one in the history of convictions has been more sure of *anything*.
No one, don't ask about her.
No! Give it to Lieutenant Kitsuragi. It's not too late -- you can still spin this as some kind of tactic.
No! Just nope. Say no to this, Harry.
No! Stop immediately. Stop dancing and apologize.
No, don't listen to him. It's madness -- just *talk*.
No, he heard you crystal clear.
No, hold on. The last thing you need in your life is more hysteric emotions. Forget it. Find something else to do.
No, it doesn't check out. Can't you see she's clearly avoiding something here?
No, it won't be fun, it'll be annoying. *You'll* be annoying.
No, it's not good. It's the opposite of that. This will let her dictate the terms of your...
No, no. You *should* do it again. It's the *last* thing he'll be expecting.
No, no. You can't let them give you the runaround.
No, they didn't.
No, you couldn't have.
No, you shouldn't. You're an officer of the law.
No-no! Stop now.
No-no, that wasn't sensationalist at all, that is a *perfectly* valid reason for someone to kill someone.
No-no, we're not starting with that. Not now. Not this time. This thought is over.
No. It can't be!
No. Not a disaster. Weave this into the story of you. Walk out of its *ruins*. Save those who still can be saved -- *I'm* on your side.
No. There was a giant insect and they *have* to understand that. You will find proof. Don't give up.
No. This is somewhere to be. This is all you have, but it's still something. Streets and sodium lights. The sky, the world. You're still alive.
No. You *can* stop. Just wade through the hell. Month after month. Year after year -- you against the nothingness. It's possible, because *time* is possible.
No.
Not *all* of us...
Not a chance.
Not a good track record of mental health in that family.
Not for yourself -- for the people of Revachol.
Not now!  Don't melt down now, you're so close. Rewind this back to the previous thought and just say that.
Not now. Stop yourself.
Not so sure about this.
Not you too...
Nothing to do with her? You'll be the judge of that.
Nothing you can say would make you feel any better now...
Notice how it came together without casting too much suspicion on *Klaasje* -- it was nice and *Ruby-centric* in the end.
Notice how this hasn't come up at all -- even Hardie and his boys didn't mention it. Neither did you.
Now walk away. Forget about this. Walk away and do police work. *BURY* this in yourself and move on.
Of course she's *really* gone, what did you think.
Of course, this isn't some punch-out competition. You just want to leave a harmless bruise.
Oh god. That was a lie too. Who made the call then?
Oh no... he's definitely compromised too. It's his *hubris*. Sadly not much to do about it now.
Oh, come on -- you know better than that!
Oh, so you're an unaccountable wreck who has to be supervised? You don't *have* to take this display of authority, you can disobey!
Okay? And WHAT?!?
Or -- you could *not* do that. No one is making you.
Or he'll be *disappointed* in you.
Ouch.... That's like twenty points of pride-damage right there, buddy.
Out beyond the dull chatter, you catch the sound of a distant seabird, and of course the sound of the large stretches of canvas that cover the missing sections of wall...
Paranoid whisperings. He is not trying to humiliate you. He just doesn't know better.
Patience. Deep down, you have the mental power to keep listening. Not many would -- but you do.
Perhaps -- just a thought -- this has something to do with the hangover?
Please, don't make this more difficult for yourself. It's okay to swear. It can be healthy, even.
Professionalism is his coping mechanism.
Put it down.
Quit staring -- You're in public! There's plenty of time for that later... in the privacy of your own room.
Really? Quick, tell me what's under her jumpsuit?
Really? You sure it isn't a certain redhaired little gremlin who refers to himself in the third person?
Relax, the kid is just messing with you. You didn't actually get *thousands* of people killed.
Relax. It'll be okay.
Relax. It's okay if you don't make it today -- the bloated corpse isn't going anywhere.
Relax. Not everyone is out to trap you.
Relax. This was still good.
Relax. You'll find them sooner or later -- this will just take some time.
Remember, you're doing it for the bigger investigation -- this is important. Relax. Ask.
Right now. I'm gonna give you *one* chance.
STOP! For the love of god, don't.
Say it.
See? He didn't get it. Good job, jokemeister.
See? It's oddly moderate. Probably compromised.
See? There are plenty of ways to find meaning in life. You don't *need* booze to feel alive.
See? There's something off here. This one *must* know she's deploying flares and countermeasures.
See? There's that. You can do ballistics!
See? What can he do. You're a grown man.
See?! She isn't saying anything!
Seriously, just move on.
She cannot let you leave without having her say.
She looks back, time moves slowly. The triangles of her face rearranging into a weary smile...
She means this sincerely. Worrying won't do you any good.
She tries to hide it, but some *great doubt* is spreading within her. There is a crown slowly cracking above her head.
She tries to maintain a brave front, even though her eyes reveal the opposite.
She won't manoeuvre her way out of this one.
She would like to complain. She would like that very much. But she can't and she won't. 'Though' has gotten her this far and she's not giving it up.
She's attempting to mentally direct you towards the shelves. She only wants you to buy the goods, she doesn't care about books.
She's gone. Forget about it now. Concentrate on this.
She's not even asking you anything. It's so easy to just say...
Should he? This environment encourages one thing and one thing only -- drinking.
Should we? He's *nice*. I don't like *nice*.
Shut up! Let him think, too much is on the line, he's *not* gonna fuck it up because of you. I won't let you.
Six years and you haven't gotten over it, what the hell is wrong with you?
So he saw you... okay. So what. Don't let it divert you.
So she knows your name. That doesn't mean you're on the take. Criminals make up bogeyman stories about cops all the time.
So that's it? You don't even *want* to change?
So young. So *unbearably* young...
Soft, light brown eyes look back at you, directly into the space behind your eye sockets. You see the smoke rise from between her painted red lips. She's beautiful...
Some of them are. Some of them are nice. Others are scared.
Someone's been a very busy boy. Good on you...
Something in her is pulling toward some unknown rest state. She twitches -- like a sleep kick.
Something in him breaks.
Something is secretly gnawing at her confidence. It's not this Cuno kid, or the missing locusts, it's something else.
Something tells you: you will.
Stay on this *Miss Beaufort* topic.
Steel yourself, push on. Just ignore Theo.
Stop taking this abuse. YOU. SOLVED. THE. CASE.
Stop thinking this idiotic thought  *now* and say something normal instead.
Stop this line of thought now. You're too broken for ghosts.
Stop, you're only making it worse for him -- you never help with *anything*.
Stop. There's no need for this. It's in your power to change the subject.
Stop.
Subdue the regret. Dust yourself off, proceed. You'll get it in the next life, where you don't make mistakes. Do what you can with this one, while you're alive.
Super. Tip-top! This went pretty well all things considered.
Surely the alcohol didn't help either.
Swift, decisive action.
Take it easy. Don't overcompensate with this *course correction*. Ask questions first...
Technically not? C'mon man. Listen. You can't trust them. It's just you and me.
Thank god, okay, he's alive.
Thank god. If *he* can see, then *maybe* you're not insane.
Thank god. If he can see, then you're not insane.
That can't be healthy. What's happening here? Why do you keep coming back to this window?
That doesn't seem fair. You demonstrated some serious skills there, slipping away Sam Bo-style.
That's it. I'm calling it. Kim is beyond compromised.
That's not going to happen, I'm not letting him make it into one of his tasks. Know your limits, tie-creature!
That's right, you *should* do it again! It's the *last* thing he'll be expecting.

That's right. *Finish thought*. Just finish it and conveniently go on. She's watching you leave right now, you know that? Free as a bird on that roof. Lighting up a cigarette and thinking: am I glad Ruby's in this shit and not me!
That's right. Walk away.
That's right. You *can*. Whatever you're doing here, you can just stop it and go on with your life.
That's right.
That's the best she can manage for Andre. It's quite a lot, in truth. For her, at least.
The amount of focus it takes to predict when a ball-goat is in striking range is staggering, but it *can* be done. Focus!
The centipede is exaggerating -- people don't actually cut their own throats when they're shaving. At least not accidentally.
The damage is truly extraordinary. It's a miracle you don't shit yourself.
The eyebrow is exercising *psionic* control over you.
The last time you saw the lieutenant this upset was after that *thing you said in the church*...
The lieutenant assumes you're playing by the rules as written. But what's the point of playing if you can't make your own choices?
The lieutenant seems oddly sure he is not compromised, not psychologically at least. Is it hubris?
The lieutenant thinks you're some unaccountable wreck who needs constant supervision, but you don't *have* to listen to him. You're free to disobey.
The lieutenant's reverse psychology fourberie doesn't work on you. If you want to look like a mega-secret mid-life crisis, that's your choice.
